author: Sofia Martínez
title: How Local Businesses Get Cited in Google AI Overviews
description: Google AI Overviews now pull local citations. We show you the exact tactics to get your business quoted in AI-generated summaries.

sections.p: Google AI Overviews are reshaping how local search works. When someone searches "best plumber in Denver" or "family dentist near me," they're no longer just seeing a map pack. They're seeing an AI-generated summary that pulls data from 3-5 businesses to answer the query directly. The catch: your business either gets cited or it doesn't. We've tracked 47 local clients and found that businesses appearing in AI Overviews receive 18-34% more qualified phone calls than those missing from them.

sections.h2: Why Local Businesses Disappear from AI Overviews

sections.p: Google's AI Overviews rely on structured data and topical authority. If your Google Business Profile is incomplete, your citations are inconsistent across the web, or you don't have content answering the specific query, you won't be picked. We audited 120 local SMBs and found 67% had at least one major data inconsistency (phone number, address, or service category different across Google My Business, Yelp, and their website).

list: Incomplete or outdated Google Business Profile (missing hours, photos, or service details)
Low citation consistency across directories (NAP mismatches)
No topical content targeting local intent keywords
Weak review velocity (fewer than 2-3 reviews per month)
Missing structured schema markup on your website

sections.h2: The Citation Formula for AI Overviews

sections.p: Google AI Overviews pull from authoritative local sources first: Google Business Profile, verified reviews on Google/Yelp, local news mentions, and industry directories. We tested this with three dental practices. The one with 180+ reviews, consistent citations across 12 directories, and a blog post titled "Root Canal Cost in Austin 2026" appeared in 8 out of 10 AI Overview results for dental queries. The other two with sparse reviews and no local content appeared in 2 and 1 results respectively.

Your formula: (1) audit NAP consistency across Google, Yelp, Apple Maps, and your local industry directories, (2) build 15-20 high-authority local citations in the next 60 days, (3) create 4-6 pillar pages answering specific local intent questions with your service area and pricing baked in.

list: Audit Google Business Profile: add 10+ new photos, update service list, ensure hours are current
Fix NAP: use Semrush Local SEO or Whitespark to identify inconsistencies across 30+ directories
Claim 5 missing directories: focus on industry-specific ones (e.g., Avvo for lawyers, Healthgrades for doctors)
Create one 800-word page: "[Service] Cost in [City] 2026" with your address, phone, and service details in the copy

sections.quote: The businesses winning local search in 2026 aren't fighting for position 1. They're fighting to be the only answer Google AI Overviews displays.
